scope: compressed dKV sparse-MLA backward producer for actual TorchTitan LI boundary
read: A concrete TileLang fused-recompute dKV producer now works: it consumes real q, dO, output, lse, compressed KV, and packed group ids, recomputes sparse-MLA probabilities and scaled grad-logits inside the producer, and writes final [groups, D] dKV without materialized [S, topk, H] probability slabs in timing-only S4096 runs. It is not a performance promotion because the current all-in-one schedule recomputes QK and dO.KV once per output D-block owner.

boundary_correctness.interpretation: The BF16 MFMA recompute path agrees with the existing materialized-probability oracle to about 1.5e-2 max absolute error at S128/S512. The drift is from BF16 MFMA recompute versus the fp32 PyTorch materialization oracle, not from route packing.

baseline_materialized_input_selected_lim.read: Fast retained producer, but it consumes prebuilt per-head probabilities and scaled grad-logits.

recompute_cap512_blockd64.read: The full 512-group panel with block_d=64 does not fit once qk, dO.KV, softmax-dot, and final group reducer state are colocated.

decision.why: The mode proves the slab-free TileLang input contract and target-shape route viability, but the all-in-one CTA schedule repeats full-D probability recompute for every D-block. The best S4096 timing, 89.07 ms, is 12.89x slower than the retained materialized-input producer and far above the sparse-backward target envelope.
decision.next_kernel_step: Prototype a two-level TileLang schedule where qk, dO.KV, softmax probability, and scaled grad-logit are computed once per token/selected tile, then reused across D-block dKV accumulation. Candidate structures are token-selected outer recompute with inner D-block final atomics, or a compact P/dP tile workspace small enough to stay on-chip or stream once per selected tile. Do not pursue the current d_block-owned all-in-one recompute schedule further except as a correctness oracle.
